top_jobs.title: Intern, Data Science & Artificial Intelligence (Summer 2026)
top_jobs.description:
Description

Intern, Data Science & Artificial Intelligence

2026Internship Dates: June1, 2026- August7, 2026

AArete is one of a kind when it comes to consulting firm culture.

As an Intern at AArete, you'll have the opportunity to directly impact both team and client objectives while fostering your personal growth. We seek organized, motivated individuals excited to join our experienced consulting team and serve clients across healthcare, financial services, retail, and other industries.

AArete Interns work closely with clients to understand critical issues and develop strategies for meaningful change. You'll leverage your analytical skills, entrepreneurial spirit, and commitment to excellence. We're looking for critical thinkers with exceptional communication skills who are eager to dive deep into business challenges, data analytics, and business case development to drive project success.

The Role

In this role, you'll demonstrate your analytical abilities and support the successful execution of project goals and deliverables. You'll have the chance to make a significant impact by addressing unique business challenges in both client and internal projects.

Work You'll Do

You will work on projectsin a variety of different areas within technology, including:

-Data Architecture & Engineering

- AI & Machine Learning

-Business Intelligence & Visualization

-Data Science & Analytics

-Application Development

What you'll gain
• Professional and personal growth through a comprehensive internship program designed to develop your skills and career
• Direct impact on team and client objectives, allowing you to contribute meaningfully to real-world projects
• Hands-on experience with our experienced technology team, working on impactful projects across diverse industries
• Exposure to various sectors, including healthcare, financial services, and retail, broadening your industry knowledge
• Mentorship and guidance from experienced professionals in the field
• Networking opportunities with professionals and leaders in the industry
• Competitive compensation and valuable insights to kickstart your consulting career

Requirements

Education & Eligibility
• Current undergraduate student entering either their 3rd year of a4-yeardegree program or in the 4th year of a 5-year program(Spring 2027 Grad)
• Minimum3.0cumulative GPA
• Must be legally authorized to work in the United States without the need for employer sponsorship
• Must be able to spend the summer in our downtown Chicago office, personal preference and circumstances will be taken into consideration for any remote candidates who are unable to be on site

Skills & Abilities
• Hands-on experience with Agentic AI and Generative AI systems
• Familiarity with machine learning concepts such as classification, clustering, feature engineering, and model evaluation
• Understanding of generative AI tools and their applications in analytics and automation
• Knowledge in Agentic AI system using Strands SDK (or equivalent) and inferencing approaches
• Strong interest in pursuing a career in data science and analytics
• Demonstrated passion for continuous learning and staying current with emerging data science tools and technologies
• Ability to approach problem-solving with logical, analytical, and data-driven thinking
• Capable of understanding business objectives and using data to craft and deliver meaningful insights and solutions
• Proficient in Python, SQL, and data visualization tools (such as Power BI or Tableau), with the ability to further develop and apply these skills
• Works effectively both independently and collaboratively in project teams
• Excited to support Data Science team in developing innovative, data-driven solutions for clients

Preferred Requirements
• Degree/Major in Computer Science, Statistics, or related field
• 3.3 or higher cumulative and major GPA
• Relevant work experience, including internships, summer positions, or part-time jobs
• Active involvement in
top_jobs.match_reason: Data Science & AI consulting internship in NYC with strong alignment to candidate's analytics, Python, and project experience; no visa restrictions mentioned.

top_jobs.title: Data Analytics Intern - Business Intelligence - Remote, NE 8
top_jobs.description:
Ameritas Life Insurance Corp is looking for a Data Analytics - Business Intelligence Intern to drive the business by providing internal business stakeholders with valuable data analytics and dashboards to better enable them to meet business needs.
At Ameritas, our mission is Fulfilling Life. We do that in many ways, but especially by helping people invest in themselves by offering trusted financial products and advice. Because we believe everyone should be happy, healthy, and financially secure, we work hard to provide trusted financial products and valued guidance, including individual life and disability insurance, employee benefits, retirement planning, investments, and wealth management services.
This internship will start in January 2026. This is a hybrid role offered in either Lincoln, NE or Cincinnati, OH.
What you do:
• Process and analyze datasets to support business stakeholders.
• Develop and maintain reports and dashboards using Business intelligence tools like Power BI and Cognos
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand data needs, use cases and propose solutions.
• Troubleshoot and resolve data issues as they arise.
• Collaborate with Data Engineering and IT admin teams as needed to ensure data and reporting tasks are completed on time.
• Contribute to the documentation of processes and procedures.
What you bring:
• Must be enrolled in a college program at least half-time as defined by your institution for the entire duration of the internship studying computer science, data science, data analytics or related field.
• Able to commit to a long-term internship working full-time in the summer and part-time during the school year.

• Full-time hours: 30-40 hours per week

• Part-time hours: 15-20 hours per week

• Consistently demonstrates initiative and a professional, self-directed approach to responsibilities and an ability to meet deadlines.

• Has taken coursework related to data management, databases, AI/Machine Learning, and similar subject matter.

• Approaches situations with a natural curiosity and has a willingness to learn new tools and technologies.

• Excellent verbal and written communication and presentation skills.

• Keen analytical skills and problem-solving skills.

• Extremely strong PC and Excel skills. Intermediate to advanced experience with SQL is a plus, but not a requirement.

What we offer:
• Our company motto is "Fulfilling Life" and we take that job seriously, with a heavy commitment to volunteering and community support, employee well-being, and providing quality products and services that help our customers create a brighter future.
• Being a mutual-based organization, the return on our investment goes back into the company, to benefit our members/customers.
• Were committed to professional development, opportunity, inclusion, and diversity. Team building and collaboration are also priorities.
An Equal Opportnity Employer
Ameritas has a reputation as a company that cares, and because everyone should feel safe bringing their authentic, whole self to work, were committed to an inclusive culture and diverse workplace, enriched by our individual differences. We are an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer that hires based on qualifications, positive attitude, and exemplary work ethic, regardless of sex, race, color, national origin, religion, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity or any other characteristic protected by law.
top_jobs.match_reason: Business Intelligence & Data Analytics internship at insurance company; strong match with Tableau, Power BI, dashboarding skills; remote-friendly Washington DC location.

top_jobs.title: 2026 Strategy & Operations Analyst Intern - West & Postmates, US
top_jobs.description:
Job Description

About The Role

We are looking for current students to join Uber as a Strategic Operations Intern on the Postmates team for Summer 2026 (12 weeks)! As a Strategy & Operations Analyst intern on the Postmates team, you will help us achieve our strategic goals by conducting in-depth analysis and developing reports and insights that drive business performance. This role calls for a combination of fluency in analytical methods and languages including SQL, cross functional stakeholder engagement, and project management.

You thrive in ambiguous environments, and are a self-starter with a high degree of curiosity and track record of data based insights generation. You will have the opportunity to develop and operationalise solutions for the 'in the know' Postmates brand and audience within Uber Eats across eaters, merchants, and brand initiatives.

This program is based out of our New York, NY, San Francisco, CA and Los Angeles, CA offices.
What You'll Do
• Analyze Data - Identify trends and build meaningful recommendations
• Generate Insights - Based on the analysis, research, customer needs, feedback, and comments from our teams
• Build Reports - Help the business make better, faster decisions and identify opportunities
• Develop Solutions - Design solutions for customer and business problems, including testing
• Operationalise Projects - Bring ideas to life with cross functional stakeholders and experts
Basic Qualifications
• Rising Senior currently enrolled in a bachelor's degree in data science, business, marketing analytics, or related field with at least one semester remaining after internship completion
• Base proficiency with SQL, Excel and/or Google Sheets; including report and dashboard generation, pivot tables, and functions such as index/match or vlookup
Preferred Qualifications
• Proficient in Python, Tableau, statistical and programming languages
• Interest in financial or operational forecasting, consumer pricing, revenue management, or marketing and/or social media analytics
• Critical and structured thinking, with a natural curiosity and confidence to seek out answers, and propose solutions where there may not yet be an answer
• Strong communication and presentation skills, including experience presenting data & insights to a diverse audience
• Strong project management skills, comfortable working independently at times
• Self-motivated, solutions-orientated, with a bias for action
• Strong organization and time management skills
• An interest in the future of on-demand logistics
• Awareness of social and cultural tastes and trends
• Entrepreneurial mentality as a builder or fixer of businesses or processes

Location-Specific Preferred Experience
• New York: Experience or interest in leveraging AI tools (e.g., Google Gemini or similar) to support analysis, insights, or operational planning for large-scale initiatives
• Los Angeles: Experience or interest in content creation, social media strategy, or brand engagement initiatives
About the Team

The Postmates Operations team is responsible for growing the Postmates brand in select markets through new eater acquisition and improving engagement and retention of our active eater base. The team works cross functionally with many Postmates and Uber Eats teams, including marketing, finance, account management and product teams, to build and implement strategic operational initiatives to drive Postmates growth.

For Los Angeles, CA-based roles: The base hourly rate amount for this role is USD$27.00 per hour.

For New York, NY-based roles: The base hourly rate amount for this role is USD$30.00 per hour.

For San Francisco, CA-based roles: The base hourly rate amount for this role is USD$30.00 per hour.

For all US locations, you will also be eligible for various benefits.
top_jobs.match_reason: Strategy & Operations role at Uber focused on analysis, reporting, and business insights—good fit for candidate's analytics and business skills.
top_jobs.concern: Operations-focused rather than pure analytics; no explicit mention of technical tools or data stack.

top_jobs.title: 2026 Intern - SEI Data Office Business Analyst Intern
top_jobs.description:
Our Internship Program provides undergraduate students with a structured and meaningful learning experience designed to prepare them for future careers and help them make informed decisions about their professional aspirations. This 10-week opportunity focuses on building foundational business knowledge while helping interns understand how to effectively operate and grow in a corporate environment.

This opportunity is part of SEI's broader professional and leadership development continuum, designed to support early career talent as they explore potential career paths. While the internship does not guarantee full-time placement, it provides meaningful exposure to SEI's culture, ways of working, and development opportunities that may support future consideration for additional early career programs.

Interns will work in a hybrid model from our corporate headquarters in Oaks, Pennsylvania.

In addition to on-the-job experience, Interns can expect to:
• Build foundational professional skills and strengthen business, industry, and technical acumen through a weekly seminar series
• Develop practical workplace capabilities through interactive sessions focused on how work gets done in a corporate environment
• Participate in an ondemand summer learning plan curated by our People Development team to support business knowledge and professional development
• Gain exposure to SEI's business, teams, and culture through handson experiences and group activities
• Receive regular coaching and guidance through onetoone sessions with the program manager to support professional growth and career exploration
• Connect with members of the broader SEI workforce through career story sessions and informal coffee chats
• Build meaningful relationships with fellow interns through several events held throughout the summer
• Receive a resume review and professional headshot to support future career opportunities

Qualities successful interns bring to SEI
• Academic achievement in Business or Technology related fields of study such as finance, accounting, economics, marketing, computer science, or data analytics
• Graduating in Dec. 2026 or May/June 2027 with an undergraduate degree
• Leadership involvement in university or community organizations (e.g., charities, clubs, athletic teams, honor societies)
• A grade point average of 3.5 or above
• General awareness of artificial intelligence (AI) concepts and an interest in learning how AI can be used responsibly and effectively in a professional work environment
• Strong written and verbal communication skills
• A strong work ethic and sense of accountability
• Enthusiasm, curiosity, and active participation
• Proactiveness and willingness to learn
• Thoughtful risk-taking and openness to feedback
• Professionalism, respect, and a collaborative mindset
• Permanent work authorization in the U.S.

SEI Data Office Business Analyst Overview:

New Enterprise initiative that we started in Private Banking and working on expansion across SEI. The team works with client to propose possible solutions for their stated problems. Upon approval of a solution the team working on the development, testing, and delivery of the application code. The team members participate various client discussion with stakeholders at all levels.

SEI Data Office Business Analyst Intern:

We are seeking a Business Analyst Intern to support the SEI Data Office and Professional Services engagements, with a focus on technology delivery and project execution. This role is designed for an early-career candidate who is eager to learn how enterprise platforms are delivered to clients and how business needs translate into clear requirements and actionable plans. The intern will work closely with consultants, delivery leads, and platform teams to help document and validate requirements, analyze current-state and future-state business processes, and support successful client implementations. top_jobs.title: Kearney Business Analyst Intern 2026
top_jobs.description:
Description

As a business analyst intern at Kearney, you will work alongside a dedicated and diverse set of people and contribute to consulting project teams as we offer honest advice and practical guidance to our clients. With the freedom to be yourself and recognition for what you bring to the table, put your purpose into action and join us as we help build a future that works for everyone, everywhere - as a Kearney Original.

What to expect
• Gain unique hands‑on experiences as you broaden your knowledge and experience in an open, agile, and collaborative environment.
• Ensure the success of project workstreams by completing tasks such as gathering and analyzing data and conducting competitor assessment studies.
• Support in the creation of recommendations and implement co‑created solutions for clients across a range of industries and service practices.
• Take advantage of structured on‑the‑job learning and development opportunities, including a dedicated mentor who will provide guidance throughout your internship.

Who you are

After nearly 100 years, we know this business is fundamentally about making connections‑between facts, figures, insights, strategies, tools, technologies, and above all, people. That's why we look for collaborative, insightful, and inquisitive problem‑solvers who do not accept the first thing in front of them and who are always unapologetically themselves.

We want to hear from you if you are:
• Ready to share your ideas and contribute as soon as you join a team
• Analytically inclined and enjoy solving problems
• Able to prioritize and are a doer by nature
• In your final year of your master or undergraduate degree or fresh graduate
• Fluent in English and Polish

Interview process

If your application is successful, you will be invited to participate in our recruitment process, which consists of:
• Recruiting Test: 60‑min online test (similar to GMAT). Check out our Mock test available here .
• Interviews: We have two rounds of interviews. Each round consists of two case and behavioral interviews. Follow along with this simple guide and learn how to prepare.

Questions?

Contact

Equal Employment Opportunity and Non-Discrimination

Kearney prides itself on providing a culture where our employees belong and thrive equally, which means our people feel comfort, confidence, and joy as they do great things for our firm, our colleagues, and our clients. That's why Kearney is committed to building a diverse workforce and inclusive environment. Kearney is an equal opportunity employer; we recruit, hire, train, promote, develop, and provide other conditions of employment without regard to a person's gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, race or ethnicity, religion, age, national origin, disability, marital status, pregnancy status, veteran status, genetic information, or any other differences consistent with applicable laws. This includes providing reasonable accommodation for disabilities or religious beliefs and practices. We encourage everyone to apply, including those who may not feel historically represented in consulting.

#J-18808-Ljbffr

top_jobs.title: State Street - Global Delivery - Alpha Operations Summer Internship 2026
top_jobs.description:
Job Description
Who we are looking for
In this role, you will join the Alpha Operations team to work with State Street individuals from around the globe to support premier investment management clients. You will have responsibility to contribute to day-to-day Investment Operations in a fast-paced, flexible and exciting environment.

Why this role is important to us
You will be the first line of defense in surfacing any potentially risky activity, and will work collaboratively with your team members to ensure data accuracy. Conduct high-level analysis in specific areas of expertise, identify and resolve work-processing issues, make suggestions to streamline operations and implement. Join this growing high-visibility team focused on building innovative solutions to revolutionize how State Street services our customers.

What you will be responsible for
As Summer Intern you will
• Support the inquiries from clients, custodians and brokers to ensure timely effective processing of requests.

• Prepares, completes, and reviews standard client reporting.

• Performs transaction processing tasks within the business unit as necessary to support processing routines.

• Consults with peers to optimize the operating model.

• Oversees and trains cross-cultural team daily processing.

What we value
These skills will help you succeed in this role
• Working knowledge of Excel and other Microsoft Office applications and experience with Bloomberg is a plus.

• Highly self-driven sense of motivation.

• Naturally curious and passionate.

• Strong accuracy aptitude and detail orientation.

• Strong verbal written and interpersonal communication skills.

• Exposure to SQL or other coding languages is beneficial.

Qualifications
• Bachelor’s degree in finance, Accounting, Economics, or Business Management.

• Strong financial accounting knowledge and proficiency.

• Knowledge of Domestic and International financials; Middle/Back or Front Office Operations, Trade lifecycle mechanics, Fund Accounting and Administration, Custody/Accounting systems, Investment Operations, etc.

About State Street
What we do: State Street is one of the largest custodian banks, asset managers and asset intelligence companies in the world. From technology to product innovation, we are making our mark on the financial services industry. For more than two centuries, we have been helping our clients safeguard and steward the investments of millions of people. We provide investment servicing, data & analytics, investment research & trading and investment management to institutional clients. We genuinely believe our employees’ diverse backgrounds, experiences and perspectives are a powerful contributor to creating an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive and reach their maximum potential while adding value to both our organization and our clients. We warmly welcome candidates of diverse origin, background, ability, age, sexual orientation, gender identity and personality. Another fundamental value at State Street is active engagement with our communities around the world, both as a partner and a leader. You will have tools to help balance your professional and personal life, paid volunteer days, matching gift programs and access to employee networks that help you stay connected to what matters to you. State Street is an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer.

Salary Range:
$41,600 - $65,000 Annual
The range quoted above applies to the role in the primary location specified. If the candidate would ultimately work outside of the primary location above, the applicable range could differ.

Employees are eligible to participate in State Street’s comprehensive benefits program, which includes: our retirement savings plan (401K) with company match; insurance coverage including basic life, medical, dental, vision, long-term disability, and other optional additional coverages; paid-time off including vacation, sick leave, short term disability, and family care re

remaining_jobs.title: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning - Summer 2026 Intern (US Person Required)
remaining_jobs.description:
Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning Intern

Join a team recognized for leadership, innovation and diversity

A variety of soft skills and experience may be required for the following role Please ensure you check the overview below carefully.

When you join Honeywell, you become a member of our global team of thinkers, innovators, dreamers, and doers who make the things that make the future. That means changing the way we fly, fueling jets in an eco-friendly way, keeping buildings smart and safe and even making it possible to breathe on Mars.

Working at Honeywell isn't just about developing cool things. That's why our employees enjoy access to dynamic career opportunities across different fields and industries.

Are you ready to help us make the future?
About The Role:

Join a team that designs, develops, and integrates highly complex software applications within Honeywell. As an Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning Intern here at Honeywell, you will have the opportunity to gain hands-on experience and contribute to cutting-edge AI projects. You will work closely with our team of talented AI engineers and researchers to develop and implement innovative solutions. This internship will provide you with valuable insights into the field of AI and machine learning, and you will have the chance to apply your knowledge in a real-world setting.
What You Will Learn:
• Model, build and test AI software to ensure it can take large swatch of data and achieve desired results
• Use Python, JavaScript and C++ to create a faster, more capable AI
• Evaluate statistics, data and algorithms to increase productivity of assets, people and processes in industrial applications/use cases
• Work with team of machine-learning and data engineers to develop seamless AI and integration
• Stay current on AI knowledge, trends, and regulations
• Collect, organize, and present progress with team leadership and stakeholders

Location: This position is available in multiple locations across the United States.

The exact worksite location will be selected based on business requirements and the candidate's relevant experience and qualifications.

Pay Range: The hourly base range for this position is $20.00/hr - $42.00/hr. Please note that this salary information serves as a general guideline. Honeywell is currently unable to consider applicants who would require current or future sponsorship for employment authorization for this position. Candidates must be authorized to legally work in the United States without current or future sponsorship requirements.

We Value:

• Strong interpersonal and communication
